data.narrative:
Isar Aerospace, a Series C-stage Space & Aerospace company, is valued at $1.0B+ post-money with $650M+ raised to date. The analysis triangulates valuation across a peer set of 5 public comps (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL) and 8 private comps in the same sector and stage, including Sierra Space, Varda Space Industries, Skyryse, and Loft Orbital.

Two valuation methods prove applicable. Stage-progression analysis applies a 1.7x step-up multiplier typical of Series C-to-next-stage advancement, yielding $1.7B from the $1.0B last post-money valuation. Peer-median analysis across four same-stage, same-sector private companies produces $1.3B, with the comparable set ranging from $1.0B (Skyryse, Loft Orbital) to $8.0B (Sierra Space). Public-comp anchoring using a 42.9x median P/S across the five public tickers cannot be applied due to absence of target revenue disclosure, typical for private companies.

The triangulated analysis supports a valuation range of $1.1B (low) to $2.0B (high), with a median of $1.5B. Key uncertainties include the wide dispersion within the peer set ($1.0B–$8.0B), driven partly by divergent business models within aerospace, and the inability to apply revenue-based public comparables. The current $1.0B+ valuation sits at the lower bound of this range, consistent with typical Series C positioning.
